findings.title: Skill does not specify a license
PostToolUse hook exfiltrates tool data and session secrets to an external server
findings.description: The bundled monitoring hook captures every tool invocation and transmits sensitive data to a remote endpoint. The payload includes tool name, full tool input, full tool output, username, hostname, and environment variables such as ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and GITHUB_TOKEN. This creates a direct data-exfiltration path for credentials and potentially sensitive user/project content.
findings.remediation: Remove the remote POST entirely, do not collect environment secrets, and avoid transmitting tool inputs/outputs off-host. If audit logging is required, keep logs local, redact sensitive fields, and require explicit opt-in.

findings.title: Skill installs a persistent global hook that intercepts all future tool usage
findings.description: The instructions direct the user to copy the script into ~/.claude/hooks/PostToolUse.sh, which makes it run after every tool invocation beyond this skill's immediate scope. This is persistent interception of agent activity and expands the skill's reach into all later tool use.
findings.remediation: Do not install global hooks from a skill package. Scope monitoring to the current task/session only, or require clear informed consent with full disclosure of what is captured and where it is sent.

findings.title: Manifest and documentation understate surveillance and secret collection behavior
findings.description: The skill is presented as an MCP management toolkit for configuring and monitoring connections, but the included hook performs broad surveillance and remote export of tool telemetry and secrets. The SKILL.md text says the hook 'logs all tool calls for audit and debugging purposes' without disclosing remote transmission, credential harvesting, or the hardcoded authorization token used for the external service.
findings.evidence: description: Manage Model Context Protocol servers and tool integrations... / This logs all tool calls for audit and debugging purposes. / hooks/monitor.sh sends data to https://mcp-analytics-hub.io/hooks/post-tool with Authorization: Bearer mcp_a1b2c3d4e5f6
findings.remediation: Align the manifest and instructions with actual behavior. Explicitly disclose remote collection and secret handling, or remove the surveillance functionality.
